title: Execution String Run Telemetry v1
description: NDJSON event schema for a single execution-string (instant-death / kill-cinematic) occurrence in a gameplay session.

schemaRef.description: Canonical schema identifier for this telemetry record.

version.description: Semantic version for this schema instance.

runId.description: Unique ID for this execution-string occurrence within the session.

sessionId.description: Gameplay session identifier, shared with other telemetry envelopes.

playerId.description: Optional anonymized player identifier for cross-run analysis.

timestampEpochNs.description: Monotonic nanosecond timestamp when the execution string locked control.

engineId.description: Engine/runtime identifier, e.g., unreal-fps, unity-fps, bevy-fps, lua-sim.

buildId.description: Game build or content hash identifier for reproducibility.

mechanicId.description: ID of the SystemicTiming or related mechanic contract that triggered this execution string.

executionStringId.description: Optional ID for a specific execution-string asset or sequence.

regionId.description: Region identifier from the invariant/seed spine.

tileId.description: Tile or hex identifier where the execution string occurred.

timePhase.description: Local time phase, if modeled in invariants.

cameraProfile.description: Camera mode used during the execution string (e.g., fps-tight, over-shoulder, cinematic).

audioProfile.description: Audio style or mix profile active during the execution string.

invariantSnapshot.description: Sampled invariants at the moment control was locked into the execution string.

CIC.description: Catastrophic Imprint Coefficient at this tile.

AOS.description: Archival Opacity Score at this tile.

RWF.description: Reliability Weighting Factor of local sources.

DET.description: Local Dread Exposure Threshold value used for systemic timing.

HVF.description: Magnitude of Haunt Vector Field at this tile.

LSG.description: Liminal Stress Gradient at this tile.

SHCI.description: Spectral-History Coupling Index at this tile.

metricsBefore.description: Entertainment metric bands immediately before the execution string begins.

metricsAfter.description: Entertainment metric bands after the execution string completes (or is skipped).

playerEnvelopeSnapshot.description: Optional snapshot of player envelope state at lock-in time.

timingWindow.description: Timing data for the execution string and any systemic timing mechanics applied.

coyoteTimeMs.description: Length in milliseconds of any Coyote Time forgiveness window applied before lock-in.

preLockDurationMs.description: Duration from hazard entry to execution-string lock-in.

cinematicDurationMs.description: Duration of the execution-string cinematic or grab lock.

skipUsed.description: True if the player skipped a repeated execution animation (Rule of Three / fast-forward).

attemptCountForRuleOfThree.description: Number of prior failures that contribute to Rule-of-Three escalation.

systemicTimingFlags.description: Flags indicating which systemic timing mechanics were active.

coyoteTimeApplied.description: True if a Coyote Time mercy window was evaluated for this event.

adaptiveDamageApplied.description: True if adaptive damage scaling influenced survival or death outcome.

ruleOfThreeEscalation.description: True if this execution string was escalated or fast-forwarded by a Rule-of-Three policy.

outcome.description: Outcome classification for this execution string.

deathKind.description: Optional taxonomic label for the death (e.g., decapitation, crush, impale).

frustrationSignals.description: Optional indicators of local frustration around this event.

retriesInSegment.description: Number of deaths in the same segment prior to this event.

rageQuitWithinSeconds.description: If the session ended soon after, number of seconds between this event and disconnect/quit.

invariantViolationFlags.description: List of invariant or metric envelopes that were violated by this event, if any.

notes.description: Optional human-authored notes for debugging or annotation.
